# VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0009 - QUIC RFC 9000 MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA Runtime Credit Publication Verification

## Scope

This verification covers active-runtime protected MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA publication after supported application reads consume receive-side bytes, confirms no credit frames are emitted when no bytes are consumed, keeps terminal-stream MAX_STREAM_DATA suppression linked, and preserves sender/recovery loss-repair linkage for the protected MAX_* packets.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0006
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0007
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P1-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S4P2-0005
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0015
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0016
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0017
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0018
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0019

## Verification Method

Requirement-home execution, protected-packet inspection, existing MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA frame-codec fuzz coverage, BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation, and SpecTrace render/check for the bounded active-runtime credit publication slice.

## Preconditions

- The active runtime has 1-RTT packet protection material and an active path.
- The peer has delivered readable stream bytes under the current receive limits.
- The existing frame codec can parse and format MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA frames.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the focused RFC 9000 S4P1/S4P2/S13P3 requirement-home tests.
- Run the MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA frame-codec fuzz coverage.
- Run the `QuicConnectionStreamStateBenchmarks` BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job.
- Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.

## Expected Result

The requirement-home tests show protected MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA packets carrying the current connection and stream limits after bytes are consumed, no protected credit packets for an empty read, no additional MAX_STREAM_DATA after the stream has reached its terminal receive state, existing MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA frame-codec fuzz coverage remains linked, and the updated BenchmarkDotNet Dry suite executes.

## Status

Passed locally for the focused requirement-home coverage, MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA frame-codec fuzz linkage, BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation, and SpecTrace render/check. The proof remains limited to active-runtime credit publication on the supported application-read path.

## Related Artifacts

- SPEC-QUIC-RFC9000
- ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0009
- WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0009

## Fuzz And Benchmark Scope

The existing `QuicFrameCodecFuzzSupport.FuzzMaxDataFrame` and `QuicFrameCodecFuzzSupport.FuzzMaxStreamDataFrame` coverage remains the fuzz proof for untrusted MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA parse/format boundaries. The permanent `QuicConnectionStreamStateBenchmarks` suite now includes both the credit-publishing read branch and the empty-read no-credit branch; Dry validation proves the benchmark harness executes but is not treated as a stable performance baseline.
